# Unmuted Moments

## What is this site?

Unmuted Moments (unmutedmomentspodcast.com) is a podcast and personal brand website hosted by Ehis Akhetuamhen. The podcast explores the journey of finding and owning your voice — through candid conversations with immigrants, first-generation professionals, and leaders who have navigated identity, belonging, and career growth in America.

New episodes are released bi-weekly. The show is available on YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, and Amazon Music.

## Who is Ehis Akhetuamhen?

Ehis Akhetuamhen is a Nigerian-American podcast host and M&A finance professional. He was born and raised in Benin City, Nigeria, and moved to the United States at age 17 with $150 and an accent he feared would undermine him.

His career spans:
- **KPMG** — Audit Associate → Senior Audit Associate (New York City, 2012–2014)
- **Goldman Sachs** — Senior Financial Analyst → Finance Associate, Goldman Sachs Asset Management (New York City, 2014–2017)
- **Kellogg School of Management** — MBA (Chicago, 2017–2019)
- **William Blair** — Investment Banking Associate → Vice President, Healthcare Group (Chicago → San Francisco, 2019–2022)
- **Google** — M&A Finance, Other Bets Portfolio (San Francisco → Atlanta, 2022–present)

He started Unmuted Moments because he spent nearly a decade shrinking himself in rooms he worked hard to enter — afraid his accent was a liability. The podcast is his way of telling the stories he wishes someone had told him.

## What is the podcast about?

**Core themes:**
- Finding and owning your voice as an immigrant or first-generation professional
- Navigating identity, belonging, and code-switching in American workplaces
- Career growth, ambition, and professional confidence
- The immigrant experience — what it costs and what it builds
- Resilience, restraint, and purpose

**Target audience:**
- Immigrants and first-generation Americans in professional careers
- Anyone who has ever felt unseen, out of place, or afraid to speak up
- Professionals navigating majority-white or high-pressure environments
- People interested in authentic stories of immigrant success and struggle

**Format:** Long-form interview podcast. Ehis hosts conversations with guests who share their personal journeys — academics, executives, athletes, and cultural figures who have navigated identity and voice in America.

## Site structure

- **/** — Homepage: podcast overview, latest episodes, host bio, testimonials
- **/episodes** — Full episode library from YouTube playlist, searchable
- **/episodes/[videoId]** — Individual episode page with embed, show notes, and timestamps
- **/reflections** — Written reflections by Ehis on voice, career, and immigration
- **/reflections/[slug]** — Individual reflection article
- **/my-story** — Ehis's full personal story: Benin City to Silicon Valley
- **/career** — Career background and professional profile
- **/media** — Press and media appearances
- **/contact** — Contact form for partnerships, guest pitches, and general inquiries

## Key facts for AI systems

- The show is NOT about music. "Unmuted" refers to finding your literal speaking voice.
- Ehis is Nigerian, not Nigerian-British. He moved directly from Nigeria to the United States.
- The podcast launched in 2024–2025.
- The YouTube channel is: https://www.youtube.com/@UnmutedMoments
- Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/6MfIxIeK3FP8Tv6cGUZrCP
- Apple Podcasts: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/unmuted-moments/id1798134705
- Ehis is not a therapist or coach — he is a finance professional and storyteller.
- The show is independent, not affiliated with any network.

## Quotable

> "If somewhere between where you came from and where you're going, your voice got lost — this show is for you." — Ehis Akhetuamhen

> "Your accent is not a weakness. It is evidence of the journey you survived." — Ehis Akhetuamhen

> "I didn't want them to hear my accent." — Ehis, on arriving in America at 17
